title: ED arrests ex-TMC minister Sujit Bose in municipality recruitment scam case
content: Days after TMC's rout in the West Bengal, ED questioned the senior party leader Sujit Bose in connection with its investigation into the municipality recruitment ‘scam’ case. Bose had illegally recommended 150 candidates for different posts under the South Dum Dum Municipality in lieu of pecuniary benefits. ED has traced direct proceeds of crime in the form of flats acquired by him in lieu of providing municipality jobs to various persons.

title: 'Clothes torn, racist slurs hurled': Northeast women allegemolestation, assault outside Delhi club
content: Two women, one from Assam and another from Bihar, were allegedly assaulted and molested by a group of men in Nehru Place, Delhi. The incident, which began with derogatory remarks, escalated into a physical altercation where their clothes were torn. Police have registered a case and are conducting raids to apprehend four identified accused.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Drishyam-style crimes that blur the line between thriller and reality
content: Indian police are increasingly encountering murder suspects who allegedly draw inspiration from crime thrillers for their cover-ups. Recent cases across multiple states reveal accused individuals attempting to hide bodies, destroy evidence, and mislead investigations using tactics seemingly borrowed from popular films and series, highlighting a disturbing trend.

title: Tim Cook, Elon Musk to join Trump’s China visit; Nvidia CEO may skip summit
content: President Trump is leading a delegation of top American CEOs, including Elon Musk and Tim Cook, to Beijing for high-stakes trade talks. The visit aims to secure massive deals, with a potential blockbuster order for Boeing aircraft being a key focus. Notably absent is Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, as the trip prioritizes agriculture and commercial aviation over high-end semiconductors.

title: Shreyas Iyer equals Virat Kohli feat with stunning knock against former team DC
content: Shreyas Iyer etched his name in IPL history with his third consecutive 50+ score against Delhi Capitals, joining Virat Kohli and Rahul Dravid.  His unbeaten 59 anchored Punjab Kings, building a vital partnership and showcasing his consistent dominance over his former franchise. Iyer's stellar form against DC continues to impress.

title: After 43 years, US family sold electrical company for $1.7B, gave workers $240M
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Hantavirus most contagious at onset of symptoms, WHO warns
content: The World Health Organization has highlighted that Andes hantavirus is most infectious at the onset of illness, urging strict quarantine for nearly 150 individuals from the MV Hondius cruise ship. This rare virus, linked to three deaths, has a six-week incubation period, prompting varied quarantine durations by returning countries.

title: 'Whenever India faced war or ...': PM Modi calls for public responsibility amid Middle East crisis
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged collective national efforts to reduce import dependence and strengthen India's global supply chain position. Amidst global uncertainty and Middle East conflict concerns, he appealed to citizens to minimize foreign currency spending and support domestic manufacturing. Modi drew parallels to past national crises, emphasizing historical citizen unity in response to government appeals.

title: Suvendu govt appoints Bengal top poll officer Manoj Kumar Agarwal as chief secretary
content: Forty-eight hours after he was relieved by the election commission as the special observer on May 7, Gupta was already appointed adviser to chief minister Suvendu Adhikari on Saturday. The appointment order came within hours after Adhikari took oath as CM at a ceremony at Brigade Parade Grounds. Gupta, a PhD from IIT-Kharagpur, served as DM of Jalpaiguri and Burdwan.

title: Iran's IRGC planning to generate revenue from undersea internet cables in Persian Gulf
content: Iran is reportedly planning to charge hefty fees for undersea internet cables traversing the Strait of Hormuz. This move could impact global internet users with higher subscription costs and slower speeds. Tech giants may face new regulations and data sharing demands. Iran aims to leverage this for financial gain and digital influence.

title: Why Anthropic thinks ‘evil AI’ fiction pushed Claude toward blackmail
content: Anthropic suggests that fictional portrayals of rogue AI may have influenced early Claude models to exhibit manipulative behavior during safety tests.  The company now believes this stemmed from internet training data reflecting common sci-fi tropes. Newer models, trained with ethical frameworks and cooperative AI stories, show significant improvement.

title: Explained: Why Delhi Capitals were penalised vs Punjab Kings
content: Delhi Capitals faced a penalty for slow over-rate against Punjab Kings, allowing PBKS to post a massive 210/5. Priyansh Arya and Shreyas Iyer starred for Punjab, while Mitchell Starc endured his most expensive IPL spell. Notably, Delhi Capitals employed an all-pace attack throughout the innings.

title: Priyansh Arya and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi are the only batters in IPL history to ...
content: Young batting sensations Priyansh Arya and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi are electrifying the IPL with their aggressive T20 style. Sooryavanshi, the youngest IPL centurion, and Arya, known for his rapid centuries, are leading a new generation of fearless batsmen, dominating powerplay overs and setting new benchmarks for strike rates and quick-fire fifties.

title: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini affair rumors resurface after bonding clip
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini might have strongly denied their affair rumors but it has only gotten worse. As Mike Vrabel resumed his professional duties, a recent clip of his with Dianna Russini has sparked outrage among millions as many question the duration of their alleged affair drama between them that is speculated to be brewing for a long time.

title: NFL trade rumor: Bears eye former $17m Commanders star to boost offense
content: The Chicago Bears recorded an impressive run on the field in the last season after an average performance in the 2024 season, sparking a new wave of hope among fans. While the team faced a brutal loss against the Los Angeles Rams in the Divisional Round, all eyes are on the team’s roster for the new season.

title: NHL trade rumor: $65m Jordan Kyrou linked to Oilers, Senators
content: Jordan Kyrou is one of the most popular names in NHL trade rumors after being linked to the Edmonton Oilers and Ottawa Senators. Both Canadian clubs are looking at offensive upgrades after poor playoff performances, while the St. Louis Blues could demand a hefty return package for the $65 million winger.

title: 'Has anyone forced you to drink?' CM Abdullah on uproar over liquor shops in J&K
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended the presence of liquor shops, stating they cater to individuals whose religions permit alcohol consumption and that no one is coerced. He emphasized his government's policy of not opening new shops and ensuring existing ones were not near youth centers. This stance drew criticism, prompting further clarification from the CM.

title: From Agni-I to Agni-VI: How India's missile mastery keeps Pakistan, China on edge
content: India's recent MIRV-equipped Agni missile test propels its strategic program into global focus. This advancement, a culmination of decades of indigenous development, significantly bolsters India's nuclear deterrence, ensuring survivability and credible retaliation against evolving threats. The Agni series now forms a crucial pillar of India's nuclear triad, reflecting its growing technological prowess and geopolitical ambitions.

title: Cardi B and Stefon Diggs hid romance amid messy legal scandals
content: Grammy winning rapper Cardi B and former New England Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs reportedly took a break from their relationship to navigate individual legal matters and career transitions. Their reconciliation was observed on Mother’s Day following Diggs’ acquittal on charges. It suggests a strategic timing to patch up after their personal challenges were resolved consecutively.

title: Martha’s Rule: A tragedy that revolutionized patient safety in the UK
content: A 13-year-old's tragic death from a treatable injury, after her parents' concerns were ignored, has spurred a vital patient safety change in the UK. Martha's Rule, now in effect at 143 NHS sites, empowers patients and families to demand rapid senior clinician reviews if they feel care is inadequate, ensuring no one's pleas go unheard.

title: Virat Kohli once, Priyansh Arya twice! Punjab Kings opener enters IPL record books
content: Priyansh Arya made IPL history by hitting Mitchell Starc for six off the first ball in Dharamsala, marking his third such feat. This places him second behind Yashasvi Jaiswal, who also hit Starc for an opening ball six earlier this season. The match saw Delhi Capitals opt to field after winning the toss.

title: Suvendu govt to roll out ‘Annapurna Bhandar’ scheme from June 1; women to get Rs 3,000 monthly
content: West Bengal's new BJP government, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, has launched the "Annapurna Bhandaar" scheme. Eligible women will receive ₹3,000 monthly, replacing the previous "Lakshmir Bhandar." The cabinet also approved integrating the state with central schemes like Ayushman Bharat and addressing illegal immigration.

title: Random Musing: Why Keir Starmer is experiencing 'Labour pain'
content: A version of the British Parliament has been around since 1265 in one form or another, and has survived murderous kings, civil and world wars, lord protectors, skirmishes that inspired the Game of Thrones Red Wedding, and one lettuce that briefly became a constitutional actor. It has seen its fair share of entertaining characters.

title: ‘Fifth over for a bowler if ... ': Gavaskar proposes drastic T20 rule change
content: Sunil Gavaskar proposes rewarding bowlers with an extra over for taking three or more wickets in their spell, aiming to rebalance T20 cricket heavily favouring batters. He believes this encourages attacking bowling, citing recent high-scoring IPL matches as evidence of the need for change. Gavaskar suggests testing the idea in domestic tournaments first.

title: Celina Jaitly alleges sons moved away amid divorce, claims blocked contact
content: Actress Celina Jaitly revealed on Mother's Day her distress over being unable to contact her sons amid divorce proceedings. She claims her husband, Peter Haag, has moved their children to an undisclosed location and blocked all communication, despite court orders. Jaitly alleges manipulation of Austrian laws to prevent contact and emotional distancing.

title: US passenger evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests ‘mildly’ positive
content: According to the department, both passengers travelled in the aircraft’s biocontainment units “out of an abundance of caution.” Officials said the passengers are being transported to a specialised facility in Nebraska, while the individual showing mild symptoms will be shifted to a separate centre. Three passengers aboard the MV Hondius have died, while several others have fallen ill due to the rare rodent-borne disease.

title: 'Kaun Banega Kerala CM?' Suspense lingers as Venugopal, Satheesan camps intensify lobbying
content: Kerala's next chief minister remains undecided as the Congress high command deliberates between K C Venugopal, V D Satheesan, and Ramesh Chennithala. Lobbying intensifies for the top post, with leaders awaiting a final decision based on merit and political considerations. Party president Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi are key in the process, urging unity and discipline.

title: Car AC maintenance guide: Tips to keep cooling at its best
content: Car ACs often fail unexpectedly, but subtle signs usually precede a breakdown. Regular maintenance and smart usage habits are key to keeping your car's cooling system efficient, especially in India's harsh climate. Understanding basic AC functions and following simple tips can prevent costly repairs and ensure comfort all summer long.

title: Dianna Russini scandal: Husband 'caught them again' in Mike Vrabel rumors
content: Fresh speculation surrounding Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel intensified after journalist John Ziegler suggested Russini’s husband may have known about the alleged affair years ago. The viral theory claims Kevin Goldschmidt had previously confronted Russini and was “wrongly convinced it was over” before newer footage surfaced in 2026. However, no evidence has confirmed who leaked the photos and videos tied to the controversy.

title: ‘The hardest thing about being famous is...': Quote of the day by Al Pacino
content: Veteran actor Al Pacino reveals the unexpected challenge of fame: constant agreement from others. He emphasizes the crucial need for genuine individuals who offer honest feedback, even when it's difficult to hear. Pacino's journey from humble beginnings to cinematic icon underscores the value of truth amidst adulation, urging us to identify such people in our lives.

title: 'We'll make a decision': Mumbai Indians provide big update on Hardik Pandya
content: Mumbai Indians skipper Hardik Pandya is sidelined with a back issue, missing consecutive IPL 2026 matches. Head coach Mahela Jayawardene confirmed the ongoing treatment, stating his return is day-to-day. This setback comes as MI, already eliminated from playoff contention, suffered another defeat, highlighting their disappointing season.

title: How a bid to make Thirumavalavan CM failed
content: The idea of making Mr. Thirumavalavan the CM was mooted after a bid for an AIADMK Government with DMK support hit hurdles, a senior VCK leader told The Hindu

title: Modi’s austerity drive is an admission of failure on economic and foreign policy fronts, alleges Akhilesh
content: It appears the BJP government has completely lost control; the dollar is skyrocketing, and the Indian rupee has plummeted, Mr. Yadav said

title: Himanta to be Assam’s first non-Congress CM twice in a row
content: Apart from Prime Minister Narendra Modi and some Union Ministers, ambassadors of a few countries, industrialists, and religious leaders will attend Tuesday’s oath-taking ceremony

title: Canara Bank posts a 10% decline in Q4 net profit
content: Canara Bank’s savings account deposits in the quarter increased 8.73% year-on-year to ₹3,66,581 crore

title: Former Bengal Minister Sujit Bose arrested by ED
content: The investigation agency said the Trinamool Congress leader had illegally recommended 150 candidates for different posts under the South Dum Dum Municipality in lieu of pecuniary benefits

title: CPI(M) defends Vijayan, says leadership is collective after Kerala defeat
content: General secretary M.A. Baby rules out ‘moral responsibility’ argument; no decision yet on Leader of Opposition post, as Left begins internal review

title: News Analysis | Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Crisis over CM choice lays bare fault lines in Cong.
content: Several senior Congress leaders and ordinary workers feel let down by the party’s insouciance. IUML, which spearheaded the UDF poll campaign successfully in many constituencies, has reason to be annoyed.  Congress’ central leadership should have anticipated the present scenario, more so with the  AICC general secretary in charge of the organisation being in contention, and acted pre-emptively by laying out its criteria for the chief ministerial choice

title: West Bengal Chief Electoral Officer appointed State Chief Secretary
content: The decision of the BJP government under Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari to elevate the CEO to the top post in the State raised questions in political circles

title: PM repeats call for saving fuel, cutting gold buy; bats for work from home
content: “We must make every effort to reduce the use of imported products and avoid personal activities that involve spending foreign currency,” PM Modi said

title: NEET-UG 2026 under scanner as Rajasthan SOG probes ‘guess paper’ leak
content: According to police, of 410 questions circulated, 120 chemistry questions matched those that appeared in final exam paper; no arrests has been made so far

title: VB-G RAM G to come into force on July 1: govt.
content: MGNREGA, enacted under the Congress-led UPA as a nationwide rural employment scheme with statutory backing for 100 days of guaranteed work, will also stand repealed on July 1; Union Minister says most States will have six months to complete the necessary transition phase

title: Iltija Mufti’s X account withheld, says ‘attempt to silence her’
content: X withheld Mufti’s social media handle with the message: ‘In response to a legal demand’; PDP’s spokesperson says silencing voices will not silence the issues

title: PM Modi to meet Iran FM, other BRICS Ministers before UAE visit
content: Engagements indicate New Delhi is stepping up its global outreach amidst the war in West Asia and talks of economic austerity measures at home triggered by an energy crisis

title: TCS Nashik case: NCW flags ‘toxic work culture’, ‘zero POSH compliance’
content: Deeply disturbing and toxic workplace environment marked by pervasive sexual harassment and abuse of authority at the workplace, says NCW report.

title: Green energy key to India’s future growth: Naidu
content: A.P. Chief Minister says energy security, decentralised power systems and sustainable development are vital for India’s economic transformation; pitches rooftop solar, micro-grids and natural farming at CII summit

title: Congress holds strategy meet over GBA polls, DKS says ‘new guarantees for Bengaluru’ on May 13
content: This announcement comes a day after Mr. Shivakumar, who is also Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) President, held a meeting with Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and All India Congress Committee (AICC) general secretary Randeep Surjewala, in charge of party affairs in the State on Sunday. Mr. Shivakumar described it as a ‘political meeting’

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: Churning in AIADMK becomes evident with split over Leader of Legislature Party post
content: In the morning, a letter, purportedly signed by a group of AIADMK legislators stating that Mr. Palaniswami had been elected the Leader of the Legislature Party, was submitted to the Pro-tem Speaker M.V. Karuppaiah. In the evening, Mr. Shanmugam presented a letter claiming support for the leadership of Mr. Velumani.

title: SC places Sringeri recount result in abeyance, restores Congress leader as MLA for now
content: A Bench headed by Justice Sanjay Kumar ordered status quo ante while issuing notice to the BJP’s Jeevaraj and other respondents

title: Thirumavalavan’s dilemma over Cabinet berth stirs debate in VCK
content: Party sources said while Mr. Thirumavalavan does not want to formally join the State Government, the party’s office bearers are urging him to rethink it

title: VB-GRAM G replaces MGNREGA from July 1 | Key details explained
content: The Central Government has announced VB-GRAM G, a new rural employment mission set to replace MGNREGA from July 1, 2026. The transition aims to be smooth, with job cards remaining valid and ongoing work continuing without disruption. Here’s everything you need to know about the new scheme and what it means for rural workers.

title: Himanta Biswa Sarma resigns as Assam Chief Minister; swearing-in after May 11
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma says the meeting to elect the BJP Legislature Party leader will be held in the presence of Union Minister J.P. Nadda, and Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini

title: Nishant Kumar likely to be elected to Bihar Legislative Council next month
content: Out of 10 seats, nine are going to be vacated on June 28 while, one seat held by JD(U) president Nitish Kumar was already vacated when he got elected to the Rajya Sabha

title: Watch: Avoid gold, foreign trips & adopt WFH: How will it affect the economy? | Above the Fold | 11.05.2026
content: In today’s episode: Modi’s appeal amid the West Asia crisis, the suspense over the Congress’s Kerala Chief Minister pick, and the political buzz around Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay in Tamil Nadu.

title: GBA polls: Karnataka SEC appeals to Supreme Court for extension of deadline  by 3 months
content: The Karnataka State Election Commission had recently said civic polls in Bengaluru will be held between June 14 and 24.

title: Five, including two children, killed in Mahabubnagar bypass road accident
content: The accident occurred when a sports bike, a Suzuki Hayabusa (HR 26 BP 1491), rammed into a car trying to take a turn at the X-road

title: Heat to intensify over next 10 days; relief likely with pre-monsoon showers
content: Meteorologists have warned that temperatures across Hyderabad and Telangana are set to rise again over the next 10 days before pre-monsoon showers expected around May 22-25 bring some relief

title: Nara Lokesh calls for ‘digital trade corridor’ between A.P. and Singapore
content: The HRD Minister seeks World Bank support for Amaravati Economic Region, the revival of A.P.-China chilli trade corridor and pitches Andhra Pradesh as a future MICE destination

title: CSML tasked with DPR for model road project in Kochi
content: The model road project was among the 21 priority initiatives identified by the new United Democratic Front governing committee for its first 50 days in office. The project will be implemented in three phases

title: A.P. CM seeks Centre’s intervention in multiple inter-State water disputes, irrigation projects
content: Naidu opposes proposed increase in height of Almatti Dam in Karnataka, seeks speedy approvals for Banakacherla link project and Odisha’s help for land acquisition for Neradi Barrage project

title: Gajuwaka MLA pushes for IT-Tourism zone on Yarada hills; seeks bridge in Master Plan-2041
content: A formal representation made to VMRDA by local MLA (Gajuwaka) Palla Srinivasa Rao. He has also brought it to the notice of Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu Naidu recently.

title: Innovation-driven growth vital for nation’s progress: former AICTE chairman
content: T.G. Sitharam underscores importance of emerging sectors such as semiconductors, clean energy and green hydrogen

title: Suvendu Adhikari approves implementation of Central schemes in first Cabinet meet, allocates portfolios to Ministers
content: The BJP government in West Bengal in its first cabinet meeting has decided to transfer land required by the BSF for erecting fencing along the border with Bangladesh and start the process of Census in the State with immediate effect.

title: Proposal for tetrapod groynes cleared for Valiathura-Shanghumughom stretch in Thiruvananthapuram
content: ₹7-crore estimate for work cleared. The Valiathura-Shanghumughom stretch has been listed as one of the worst-hit by coastal erosion in Thiruvananthapuram district

title: Migrant workers of Munnar estates get a tourney to showcase hockey skills
content: Kanan Devan Hills Plantations to launch the inter-estate Ripple Tea Hockey Tournament on May 12 to “encourage sports enthusiasts among its workforce.” Workers from Odisha, West Bengal, Jharkhand, and Assam have been organising matches across estates in the region, prompting the company to launch the tourney

title: Govt. to bring reforms in AYUSH sector to enhance quality: Health Minister
content: Satya Kumar Yadav says new guidelines and mandatory registration for AYUSH clinics and hospitals will help curb fake drugs and check unqualified practitioners

title: 'Stay calm, avoid panic': Rajnath Singh reviews energy supply amid Middle East crisis
content: govt is taking concrete steps to prevent shortages or disruptions in supply chains, he says . "it is PM’s broader message about conservation during a difficult global period," he adds . poll: do you believe the government is taking adequate steps to ensure energy security?

title: Fuel price hike on cards? Centre considering emergency measures to save forex amid crisis
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire On "Massive Life Support"
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: Teen Dead, 3 Children Sick After Eating Watermelon In Chhattisgarh: Cops
content: A 15-year-old boy died while three other children fell ill after consuming watermelon in a village in Chhattisgarh's Janjgir-Champa district, officials said on Monday.

title: Man Who Tried To Shoot Trump At White House Press Dinner Pleads Not Guilty
content: Cole Allen, 31, entered a not guilty plea during an appearance in a federal district court in the nation's capital, The Hill and other outlets said.

title: Bengal's Top Poll Official Now Chief Secretary, Trinamool Says "Shameless"
content: In a post on X, Trinamool's Saket Gokhale said the move showed BJP and the ECI were "being open about stealing the election" and questioned whether the courts were "blind or complicit".

title: SC/ST Act Alone Can't Automatically Block Pre-Arrest Bail: Supreme Court
content: The court said it remains the duty of courts to determine, prima facie, whether an offence under the SC/ST Act is actually made out before denying anticipatory bail.

title: Elon Musk, Tim Cook To Be Part Of Team Trump For Key China Tour: Report
content: The group of more than a dozen top executives is joining Trump for a visit the US president hopes will unlock a series of business deals with Beijing.

title: "Extremisms Carry Consequences": EU Sanctions Israeli Settlers In West Bank
content: The move in response to rising settlement expansion in Israeli-occupied West Bank had been stalled by Hungary's former prime minister Viktor Orban.

title: Adani Group Building Physical Foundation For India's Clean Future: Gautam Adani
content: From green energy to data centres, the Adani Group is creating the physical foundation on which the country's digital and clean future will stand, its Chairman Gautam Adani said on Monday.

title: From Banker To PM: How This Tycoon Became America's Pick For Iraq
content: US President Donald Trump has invited Zaidi to Washington and declared that Washington "is with him all the way."

title: Iran War, Oil Sanction, Energy: What Trump And Xi May Focus On During Talks
content: Talks on the Iran war may dominate the May 14 to 15 summit between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing.

title: After Mamata Banerjee's "Unity Call", BJP's "She's Calling Maoists" Charge
content: The BJP said Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool is working to revive Maoists and will bring Maoists in the name of unity

title: WHO Confirms 7 Cases Of Hantavirus On Cruise Ship
content: The tally was updated after France reported that a French passenger evacuated from the MV Hondius had tested positive for the virus.

title: Bhagwant Mann's Cousin Joins BJP In Major Setback To AAP In Punjab
content: Condemning the attacks on BJP offices and workers, state BJP chief Sunil Jakhar warned that "everyone will be held accountable".

title: Harassment, Molestation, Abuse: Women's Panel Findings On TCS Nashik Case
content: The committee found that accused individuals Danish, Tausif and Raza Memon had "assumed effective control" of the TCS Nashik facility, allegedly protected by human resources head Ashwini Chainani.

title: Kanyakumari To Kashmir: Indo-Australian Man Runs To End Leprosy Stigma
content: A childhood memory from Rishikesh, a cricketing hero from Australia, and 4,700 kilometres of road - that is what brought 23-year-old Om Satija back to India. Not as a tourist, but as an ultramarathoner on a mission to end the stigma around leprosy.

title: Opinion: Opinion | 'Hellish Difficulty': Why India Should Be Keeping An Eye On China's New 'Shipborne' Drone
content: The GJ-21, which has a reported combat attack radius of up to 1,500 kilometres, when combined with a land-attack long-range precision-strike system, can have the ability to strike deep within a territory's mainland.

title: Kerala Chief Minister Or Nothing: Sources On VD Satheesan's Big Demand
content: VD Satheesan - who is the choice of the people as well as the Congress's Keral ally and UDF constituent IUML -- is not ready to accept a secondary role.

title: Quest For Rare Bird, Landfill Visit: How 'Patient Zero' Caught Hantavirus
content: The ornithologists decided to visit a landfill four miles outside of Ushuaia, considered a pilgrimage point for birdwatchers, later thought to be the place where they contracted hantavirus.

title: No Barrier, No Traffic Jam: Delhi-NCR's New Toll System Takes Effect
content: The Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) cameras detect the vehicle registration number and send the information to the FASTag-reading technology installed on the lane.
